stated.purpose: LIFE protects the environment and climate; its Clean Energy Transition strand includes the European City Facility initiative.
stated.EUCF objective: EUCF supports municipalities and local authorities to develop investment concepts for actions identified in climate and energy action plans and to build a pipeline of sustainable-energy investments.
stated.EUCF support model: EUCF provides EUR 60,000 lump sums and related capacity-building services for preparatory investment-concept development, not for the underlying capital investments.

derived.Call 7 selection pattern: Call 7 selected 71 beneficiaries across the three regional calls from 297 submitted applications (approximately 24% overall success rate).
derived.Call 7 sector pattern: Public buildings, including social-rental and public housing, were the most frequently targeted main investment sector among selected Call 7 applications in CEE and Southern Europe; innovative energy infrastructure led in Nordic countries and Western Europe.

opportunity.finance_route: EUCF is funded by the LIFE Programme; it provides support directly to local-authority beneficiaries for investment-concept preparation.
opportunity.instrument_type: Fixed lump-sum grant with capacity-building support
opportunity.region_scope: EU-27 Member States, Iceland and Ukraine, organised into Central and Eastern Europe; Nordic countries and Western Europe; and Southern Europe regional calls.
opportunity.status: Closed; Call 7 closed on 18 December 2024 and 71 beneficiaries were subsequently selected.

application_template.template_name: Letter of support to the proposed investment concept

chapter_schema.title: Letter header and proposal identification
chapter_schema.description: Location/date, investment-concept title, applicant name, and EUCF website user-zone application ID.

chapter_schema.title: Political support statement
chapter_schema.description: Statement by the mayor or other relevant political representative supporting development of the proposed investment concept within EUCF.

chapter_schema.title: Signature and contact details
chapter_schema.description: Signature and stamp; political representative name, position, department, and postal contact details.

required_fields: Name of municipality/local authority, grouping lead applicant, or eligible local public entity
Application ID in the EUCF website user zone
Name of mayor or other relevant political representative
Confirmation of support for the proposed investment concept
Political representative position and contact details

criteria.label: Eligible applicant type
criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must be a municipality/local authority, a grouping of municipalities/local authorities, or a local public entity aggregating municipalities/local authorities.
criteria.normalized_rule: Applicant must be an eligible local-authority entity or eligible grouping/aggregator.

criteria.label: Eligible geography
criteria.requirement_text: The applicant must be located entirely in an EU-27 Member State, Iceland, or Ukraine.
criteria.normalized_rule: Applicant location must be in EU-27, Iceland, or Ukraine.

criteria.label: Climate or energy plan
criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must have a SECAP or other local climate and/or energy plan with mitigation targets.
criteria.normalized_rule: A local climate/energy plan with at least one mitigation target is required.

criteria.label: Political commitment
criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must provide proof of political commitment to develop the investment concept, signed by the mayor or other relevant political representative.
criteria.normalized_rule: Submit the required signed political-support letter.

criteria.label: Monitoring commitment
criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must commit to the EUCF monitoring period of two years.
criteria.normalized_rule: Commit to two years of EUCF monitoring.

criteria.label: Ambition of the proposed investment concept
criteria.requirement_text: Applications are evaluated on the ambition of the proposed investment concept. For Calls 6 and 7, the quality threshold was revoked for this criterion.
criteria.normalized_rule: Score well on investment-concept ambition; no per-criterion quality threshold applied in Call 7.

criteria.label: Governance structure
criteria.requirement_text: Applications are evaluated on the governance structure for development of the investment concept. In Call 7, a quality threshold of 50% of total possible points applied.
criteria.normalized_rule: Achieve at least 50% of available points for governance structure.

criteria.label: Stakeholder engagement strategy
criteria.requirement_text: Applications are evaluated on the stakeholder engagement strategy. In Call 7, a quality threshold of 50% of total possible points applied.
criteria.normalized_rule: Achieve at least 50% of available points for stakeholder engagement.

criteria.label: Coherence and plausibility
criteria.requirement_text: Applications are evaluated on coherence and plausibility. In Call 7, a quality threshold of 50% of total possible points applied.
criteria.normalized_rule: Achieve at least 50% of available points for coherence and plausibility.

criteria.label: Application language and annexes
criteria.requirement_text: Full applications must be submitted in English and include a SECAP or other local climate/energy plan with mitigation targets and a letter of political commitment.
criteria.normalized_rule: Submit the English online application with both mandatory annexes.

funded_projects.title: Zero-Emission Urban Transport for Innsbruck
funded_projects.summary: Innsbruck was listed as a Call 7 beneficiary for the investment concept titled “Zero-Emission Urban Transport for Innsbruck.”

funded_project_actions.description: Prepare the investment concept titled “Zero-Emission Urban Transport for Innsbruck”; EUCF support is for the preparatory investment concept rather than direct financing of the underlying transport investment.

financial_amounts.description: Fixed EUCF lump-sum support package for the selected Innsbruck beneficiary to develop its investment concept; this is preparatory support, not capital financing for transport implementation.
Published total Call 7 budget allocated to support development of 71 investment concepts across the three regional calls.

financial_amounts.status: Expected investment indicated by selected applicants
financial_amounts.description: Aggregate expected underlying investment size reported by selected Call 7 applicants in Central and Eastern Europe; it is not EUCF grant funding or committed financing.

financial_amounts.status: Expected investment indicated by selected applicants
financial_amounts.description: Aggregate expected underlying investment size reported by selected Call 7 applicants in Nordic countries and Western Europe; it is not EUCF grant funding or committed financing.

financial_amounts.status: Expected investment indicated by selected applicants
financial_amounts.description: Aggregate expected underlying investment size reported by selected Call 7 applicants in Southern Europe; it is not EUCF grant funding or committed financing.

sources.title: European City Facility announces 71 new beneficiaries | EUCF

evidence.quote_or_summary: CINEA presents LIFE as the LIFE Programme and identifies it as the Programme for the Environment and Climate Action.

evidence.source_location: LIFE page / official EU website notice
evidence.quote_or_summary: The source is an official European Union website and presents EUCF among LIFE initiatives supporting municipalities across Europe.

evidence.source_location: Objective; About the European City Facility
evidence.quote_or_summary: EUCF supports municipalities/local authorities to develop investment concepts for actions in climate and energy plans and build a sustainable-energy investment pipeline; it is funded by LIFE.
The document states EUCF was originally set up under Horizon 2020 and is now funded by the EU LIFE Programme.

evidence.source_location: Financial Support; About the European City Facility
evidence.quote_or_summary: EUCF provides tailor-made, fast and simplified financial support in the form of EUR 60,000 lump sums and related services.
Eligible geography is EU-27 Member States, Iceland and Ukraine, with calls organised in three geographic regions.
The EUCF finances development of an Investment Concept with a fixed grant of EUR 60,000.
The published grant is a fixed EUR 60,000 amount, establishing the same minimum and maximum award.
The fixed grant is denominated in EUR.
The Call 7 page explicitly states that the call is closed and that it was the last round currently planned.
EUCF selected 71 beneficiaries from 297 applications submitted during the seventh call; the existing EUCF calls are all closed.
The captured DOCX is the EUCF letter-of-support template and contains proposal identification, political-support declaration, signature, and representative contact fields.
The official application page provides five yes/no eligibility questions, says eligibility requires YES to all, and identifies the plan and political-support letter as required annexes.
For Calls 6 and 7, the quality threshold was revoked for criterion 1, ambition of the proposed investment concept.
For Call 7, the 50% quality threshold applied to criteria two, three, and four.
For Call 7, the 50% quality threshold applied to criteria two, three, and four.
For Call 7, the 50% quality threshold applied to criteria two, three, and four.
The official Call 7 beneficiary list identifies Innsbruck, Austria, with the investment-concept title “Zero-Emission Urban Transport for Innsbruck.”
The named Innsbruck investment concept explicitly concerns zero-emission urban transport; the announcement states selected cities use funding to develop investment concepts.

evidence.source_location: Opening paragraphs; Full list of new beneficiaries
evidence.quote_or_summary: The announcement says Call 7 selected 71 new beneficiaries for its EUR 60,000 support package and lists Innsbruck among them.
Selected cities receive a EUR 60,000 support package to develop investment concepts; the announcement was published 18 July 2025.
Call 7 budget was EUR 4.26 million for 71 investment concepts.

evidence.source_location: 5.1.3 Expected investment size of selected applications
evidence.quote_or_summary: Selected Call 7 CEE applications indicated approximately EUR 993 million total expected investments.

evidence.source_location: 5.2.3 Expected investment size of selected applications
evidence.quote_or_summary: Selected Call 7 NC&WE applications indicated more than EUR 719 million total expected investments.

evidence.source_location: 5.3.3 Expected investment size of selected applications
evidence.quote_or_summary: Selected Call 7 Southern Europe applications indicated around EUR 900 million total expected investments.
The report records 297 Call 7 submissions, a 24% success rate, 71 selected applications, and region-specific leading investment sectors.

gaps.reason: LIFE is an EU-level programme; no institutional country is appropriate, and the available official sources do not define one.
The full Call 7 application was completed in the EUCF online user zone. Captured sources provide a mandatory letter-of-support DOCX template, but no downloadable full application-form template.
Captured official Call 7 sources establish the four criteria and applicable 50% thresholds but do not publish numeric criterion weights.
No Call 7 co-financing, match-funding, or contribution requirement was established in the captured official sources.
The Call 7 beneficiary announcement confirms a fixed EUR 60,000 support package but does not publish Innsbruck's requested amount.
The official announcement establishes selection and the EUR 60,000 support package, but does not establish Innsbruck's executed grant agreement, payment, or disbursement status.
The official beneficiary list identifies Innsbruck's zero-emission transport investment concept but does not publish its project-specific expected capital-investment amount.
No action-level preparation cost or underlying transport-investment cost for Innsbruck was published in the captured sources.
The regional expected-investment figures are applicant estimates; no committed, financed, or disbursed downstream capital investment was established for Call 7 or the Innsbruck concept.
No official Call 7 ranked pipeline, requested-amount table, or applicant-level fundable-amount list was found in the captured sources.
No explicit reusable-content licence was identified in the captured official pages, report, guidance, or template.
